name: wildhaven-ai-homes-twin
description: The Pre-Founder twin of Wildhaven AI Homes. The brand operates in public, in this repo, before the team incorporates. Built on the RAPP digital-organism platform; descended from kody-w/RAPP via the rappid lineage chain.
_attestation_note: When kody-w/RAPP adopts release signing, this variant's attestation will be issued by rapp's release key asserting (parent_rappid, parent_commit, child_rappid). See pages/vault/Architecture/Signed Releases and Variant Attestation.md in the parent repo.

private_companion.purpose: Operational shadow — data the twin uses to reason but never publishes. Pipeline, financial model, hiring targets, partnerships, legal drafts, founder notes, research.
private_companion.access_required: Read access to the private repo (granted to the brainstem operator and authorized collaborators).

auth._note: Anonymous requests to the URLs above return 404 (GitHub does not reveal private-repo existence). Authenticated requests with a GitHub token that has read access return the file contents. The brainstem resolves a token via WAH_PRIVATE_TOKEN env > GITHUB_TOKEN env > `gh auth token` CLI, in that order.

private_companion._note: Two access paths, both supported by utils/private_layer.py: (1) local clone at .private/ — fast, offline-capable, zero network. (2) authenticated remote fetch via raw_url_template — no clone required, works from any host with operator auth, gracefully 404s for non-operators. Local takes precedence when both are available. The private layer is never served over HTTP from this twin.
